response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a capable full-stack engineer with strong TDD and distributed systems exposure, but he is fundamentally mismatched for this role. The position's hard requirement of 8 years of C++ experience is not met — the resume shows only a brief 6-month stint writing C/C++ components at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a bachelor thesis project. His primary language stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and D, with no cloud platform leadership, no formal technical lead tenure over infrastructure teams, and no production-level cloud architecture at scale. The gap on the minimum qualifications alone makes him an unviable candidate.
strengths: Demonstrated commitment to engineering craft: TDD, pair programming, trunk-based development, CI/CD applied consistently across multiple roles
Exposure to high-throughput distributed systems (Dunnhumby: millions of HTTP req/s, async I/O, low-latency pipelines) and Kubernetes-based deployment
Active knowledge-sharing culture: organized Refactoring Dojos, delivered conference talks, built open-source testing tooling (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
concerns: C++ experience is critically absent — only ~6 months of C/C++ work documented versus the required 8 years; C++ does not even appear in his listed skills
No cloud platform or infrastructure leadership: career arc is full-stack product engineering, not compute/storage/network platform ownership
No formal technical lead or staff-level mentoring of cloud/DevOps engineers; staff titles held were individual-contributor roles, not leadership-track positions
